bc_big_ideas: Living things have features and behaviours that help them survive in their environment
Plants and animals have observable features that help them meet their basic needs
Daily and seasonal changes affect all living things

bc_standards.description: Living things have features and behaviours that help them survive in their environment
Observe and describe the features and behaviours of plants and animals
Identify how different features of plants and animals help them meet their basic needs (e.g., food, shelter, protection)
Recognize that living things grow and change over time
Describe how daily and seasonal changes affect living things
Demonstrate care and respect for living things in their local environment

csta_standards.description: Select and operate appropriate software to perform a variety of tasks, and identify hardware and software components (Computing Systems)
Use appropriate terminology in identifying and describing the function of common physical components of computing systems (Computing Systems)
Collect and present data about living things in various visual formats (Data & Analysis)
Describe and interpret data collected about living things (Data & Analysis)
Identify patterns in data about living things (Data & Analysis)
Model daily processes by following and describing algorithms (Algorithms & Programming)
Create programs with sequences of commands to accomplish simple tasks (Algorithms & Programming)
Identify ways that technology can be used to learn about living things (Impacts of Computing)
Work individually and collaboratively to create ways to share information about living things using technology (Impacts of Computing)

curriculum_notes: This mapping aligns BC Grade 1 Science Living Things curriculum with CSTA K-2 Computer Science standards. The CSTA standards focus on using technology to collect, analyze, and present data about living things, supporting scientific inquiry skills.
